cot_prefix: Okay, let's break down this question. The passage is from the perspective of someone who works from home. The main idea is that while working from home has its perks like flexibility and the ability to attend to family matters, there are also some less obvious downsides that aren't often discussed. Let me go through the options: (A) can balance her work and family life well While the passage does talk about the ability to attend to kids or go shopping, it also suggests that this flexibility comes at a cost, as the writer often has to work late to make up for the time spent on these activities. The passage doesn't definitively say that the writer balances work and family life well, it just describes the challenges. (B) is quite satisfied with her present work The passage starts by saying the writer loves being able to work from home and make her own hours. However, the rest of the passage focuses more on the challenges of this arrangement. The writer does express appreciation but also highlights the difficulties. This option seems partially correct but not the main point of the passage. (C) presents some unknown facts of working from home This option aligns well with the passage's main theme. The writer starts by acknowledging that people perceive working from home as ideal, but then goes on to reveal some of the less-discussed challenges like having to work late hours to compensate for family time and being constantly available for work communication. The writer explicitly says "I do want to share a few things about telecommuting that few home-based people want to tell you". (D) complains about the disadvantages of working from office This is incorrect because the writer is not talking about working in an office.
